_provenance.extracted_by: in-session Claude Code sub-agent (no API call), 2026-06-27
_provenance.purpose: The FULL problem_07 Q1 proof (contraction-establishment + Banach convergence) as a rich grounded dgroup, for multi-prior set-cover decomposition (retrieval.decompose). Shares functor + entity vocabulary with retrieval/library/proof_theorems.json so each theorem's statement appears as a sub-structure of the proof; the residual (SENSITIVITY + ITERATION_COMPLEXITY) is the paper's specific contribution over standard contraction theory.

target.name: problem_07-q1-full
target.text: By the first-order optimality conditions of the retraining mapping, grad L(T(theta); theta) = 0 and grad L(T(theta'); theta') = 0. Applying the gamma-strong convexity of L gives gamma ||T(theta) - T(theta')|| <= || grad L(T(theta);theta) - grad L(T(theta');theta) ||. Rewriting the gradient difference as an expectation discrepancy under shifting distributions D(theta) vs D(theta') and applying the Kantorovich-Rubinstein theorem with the beta-Lipschitz gradient bounds it by beta * W1(D(theta), D(theta')). Invoking the epsilon-sensitivity condition W1(D(theta),D(theta')) <= epsilon ||theta - theta'|| yields the contraction ||T(theta) - T(theta')|| <= (epsilon beta / gamma) ||theta - theta'||. Let kappa = epsilon beta / gamma. Since kappa < 1, by the Banach Fixed-Point Theorem T has a unique fixed point theta_star. By induction ||theta_t - theta_star|| <= kappa^t ||theta_0 - theta_star||, so the sequence converges at a linear rate, and solving kappa^t ||theta_0 - theta_star|| <= delta gives the iteration count t >= ln(||theta_0-theta_star||/delta)/ln(1/kappa).

groundings.the_seq: the sequence converges at a linear rate
